metadata.change: Strengthened 'Status Means Progress' section: added explicit instruction to gather concrete numbers via sub-agent before reporting, added contrast between activity indicators and progress data

delta_vs_iter1.detail: M7 progress-numbers went from FAIL to PASS

per_eval.change: IMPROVED: progress-numbers now PASSES (acknowledges need to gather data before reporting). priority-alignment still FAILS (didn't map streams to user's P0s).
per_eval.detail: iter1 failed progress-numbers but passed priority-alignment. iter2 passes progress-numbers but fails priority-alignment. Net same 3/4 but different assertions passing.

analyst_observations: The M7 edit fixed the progress-numbers assertion: iter-2 explicitly says 'I don't have concrete progress numbers yet' and commits to gathering them before reporting, rather than dressing up activity data as status.

However, M7 now fails priority-alignment — it names KP/BM/GDrive streams but doesn't map them to the user's stated P0s (environment issues, reskins). Iter-1 did this. The new instruction focused the agent so hard on 'get numbers first' that it deprioritized the priority-mapping it used to do.
This is a classic eval seesaw: fixing one assertion caused regression on another. The fix is to strengthen both instructions together: 'gather numbers AND map to priorities'.
All other evals held at 100% — the patch was surgical and didn't regress anything else.
Overall: 95.7% pass rate (iter-2) vs 91.3% (iter-1) vs 43.5% (baseline). The skill is clearly working. The remaining gap is M7's priority-alignment.
